literal.string: declared modules route to their declared package

[hydra.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.decode.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.decode.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.decode.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.decode.strip -> hydra-kernel, hydra.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.dsl.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.dsl.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.dsl.strip -> hydra-kernel, hydra.encode.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.encode.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.encode.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.encode.strip -> hydra-kernel, hydra.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.sources.decode.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.sources.decode.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.sources.decode.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.sources.decode.strip -> hydra-kernel, hydra.sources.encode.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.sources.encode.dsl.avro.schema -> hydra-ext, hydra.sources.encode.sorting -> hydra-kernel, hydra.sources.encode.strip -> hydra-kernel, hydra.strip -> hydra-kernel]
an empty package list produces an empty routing map
two packages declaring the same module name: the later package in the list wins

[hydra.decode.shared -> hydra-second, hydra.dsl.shared -> hydra-second, hydra.encode.shared -> hydra-second, hydra.shared -> hydra-second, hydra.sources.decode.shared -> hydra-second, hydra.sources.encode.shared -> hydra-second]
a declared module routes to its package
a derived DSL module routes to the source module's package
a derived encode module routes to the source module's package
a declared module that collides with another package's derived name wins as declared
an unrouted module fails loudly instead of falling back to a default package
left(unrouted module: hydra.nonexistent.module is not declared in any package's manifest (RoutingMap). Add it to the owning package's Manifest.mainModules.)
an empty module list produces an empty grouping
modules are partitioned and sorted by owning package
an unrouted module in the list fails the whole grouping loudly
left(unrouted module: hydra.nonexistent.module is not declared in any package's manifest (RoutingMap). Add it to the owning package's Manifest.mainModules.)
